getAnalyticsBotflowDivisionsReportingtur.description: Returns the reporting turns for the specified flow, filtered by the clients divisions and grouped by session, in reverse chronological order from the date the session was created, with the reporting turns from the most recent session appearing at the start of the list. It is expected that the client will URL encode the request URI once only. For pagination, clients should keep sending requests using the value of 'nextUri' in the response, until it's no longer present, only then have all items have been returned. The 'nextUri' value in the response is already URL encoded (so it doesn't need to be encoded again). Note: resources returned by this endpoint are not persisted indefinitely, as they are deleted after approximately, but not before, 10 days.

It is expected that the client will URL encode the request URI once only. The 'nextUri' value in the response is already URL encoded (so it doesn't need to be encoded again). Note: resources returned by this endpoint are not persisted indefinitely, as they are deleted after approximately, but not before, 10 days.

patchUserGeolocation.description: The geolocation object can be patched one of three ways. Option 1: Set the 'primary' property to true. This will set the client as the user's primary geolocation source.  Option 2: Provide the 'latitude' and 'longitude' values.  This will enqueue an asynchronous update of the 'city', 'region', and 'country', generating a notification. A subsequent GET operation will include the new values for 'city', 'region' and 'country'. postWebchatGuestConversations.description: This endpoint is deprecated. Please see the article https://help.genesys.cloud/articles/deprecation-removal-of-acd-web-chat-version-2/. This endpoint will create a new ACD Chat conversation under the specified Chat Deployment. The conversation will begin with a guest member in it (with a role=CUSTOMER) according to the customer information that is supplied. If the guest member is authenticated, the 'memberAuthToken' field should include his JWT as generated by the 'POST /api/v2/signeddata' resource; if the guest member is anonymous (and the Deployment permits it) this field can be omitted. The returned data includes the IDs of the conversation created, along with a newly-create JWT token that you can supply to all future endpoints as authentication to perform operations against that conversation. After successfully creating a conversation, you should connect a websocket to the event stream named in the 'eventStreamUri' field of the response; the conversation is not routed until the event stream is attached.

The OAuth Grant/Client is required in order to create an authentication token and gain access to PureCloud. 
The preferred authorizedGrantTypes is 'CODE' which requires applications to send a client ID and client secret. This is typically a web server. 
If the client is unable to secure the client secret then the 'TOKEN' grant type aka IMPLICIT should be used. This is would be for browser or mobile apps. 
If a client is to be used outside of the context of a user then the 'CLIENT-CREDENTIALS' grant may be used. In this case the client must be granted roles 
via the 'roleIds' field.

getQualityAgentsActivity.summary: Gets a list of Agent Activities
getQualityAgentsActivity.description: Each item on the list shows one agent's evaluation activity comprised of the number of evaluations and the highest, average, and lowest standard and critical scores, as well as a sub list showing the number and average score of evaluations for each evaluator for that agent.  evaluatorUserId, startTime, and endTime are all filtering criteria. If specified, the only evaluations used to compile the agent activity response will be ones that match the filtering criteria. agentUserId, name, group, and agentTeamId are all agent selection criteria. criteria.  If one or more agent selection criteria are specified, then the returned activity will include users that match the criteria even if those users did not have any agent activity or evaluations that do not match any filtering criteria.  If no agent selection criteria are specified but an evaluatorUserId is, then the returned activity will be only for those agents that had evaluations where the evaluator is the evaluatorUserId.  If no agent selection criteria are specified and no evaluatorUserId is specified, then the returned activity will be for all users

getQualityEvaluationsQuery.summary: Queries Evaluations and returns a paged list
getQualityEvaluationsQuery.description: Query params must include one of conversationId, evaluatorUserId, agentUserId or assigneeUserId. When querying by agentUserId (and not conversationId or evaluatorUserId), the results are sorted by release date. Evaluations set to 'Never Release' are omitted in this case. When querying by evaluatorUserId or conversationId (including when combined with agentUserId), the results are sorted by assigned date. NOTE: The count for total and pageCount might not be accurate when querying for a large number of evaluations. nextUri, if present, will indicate that there are more evaluations to fetch. The evaluation entities contained in the response might only contain a subset of all the properties listed below. It is often because a given property's value has not yet been populated or is not applicable in the current state of the evaluation. It might also be because the missing property in the response was not requested by the user.

postQualityEvaluationsSearch.summary: Search evaluations based along specified criteria
postQualityEvaluationsSearch.description:
Search Rules:
1. Time Range
   - Time Range: Max 3 months (required)
2. Question Group Level Query: Use at least one field containing 'questionGroup' in name + exactly one questionGroupId
3. Question Level Query: Use at least one field containing 'question' in name + exactly one questionId
4. Mixed Queries: questionId alone is sufficient
5. Search Logic:
   - Multiple criteria: AND operation
   - Multiple values per criterion: OR operation
   EXAMPLE: (agentId₁ OR agentId₂) AND (evaluatorId₁ OR evaluatorId₂)
5. Aggregations:
   - Omit or set pageSize = 0
   - Choose: multiple aggregations OR single aggregation with multiple sub-aggregations
   - To aggregate against question fields, one must query by either a questionId OR a single top level TERM questionId aggregation AND query by a single formID or questionGroupId, or list of questionIds
   - To aggregate against question group fields, one must query either a questionId/questionGroupId OR a single top level TERM questionGroupId aggregation AND query by a single formID or list of questionGroupIds

coaching.get.appointments.summary: All coaching appointments — session details, attendees, status (Scheduled/Completed/Cancelled), and associated documents.

analytics.post.users.details.jobs.summary: Submit async user presence and routing-status detail job — use for large time-window agent activity analysis.

analytics.get.user.details.job.status.summary: Poll status of an async user details job — returns QUEUED, RUNNING, FULFILLED, or FAILED.

analytics.get.user.details.job.results.summary: Fetch paged results from a completed user details job — cursor-paged user presence and routing-status segments.

analytics.get.reporting.exports.summary: All configured analytics reporting export jobs — schedule, format (CSV/PDF), recipients, and last-run status.

combinations._description: Named investigation recipes and reporting playbooks — composed sequences of catalog datasets for specific analytical goals. Each recipe documents dataset order, join keys, diagnostic signals, and the executive metrics it surfaces. Recipes are consumed by Genesys.Ops investigation cmdlets and by application-layer reporting pipelines.

single-conversation-investigation.description: Complete forensic investigation of one conversation — identity, timeline, recordings, wrapup, S&TA sentiment/categories, evaluations, and CSAT survey. Used by QA analysts, supervisors, and voice engineers to understand everything that happened in a single interaction.

steps.description: Full participant and segment timeline with all duration metrics (tTalk, tHeld, tAcw, tHandle)

steps.provides: mediaType, state, originatingDirection, participantRoster, ANI, DNIS
steps.description: Base conversation object with media type, state, and full participant roster
Wrapup code selected by each agent; requires iterating over agent participants from the seed

steps.provides: callLeg details, hold events, ANI/DNIS routing path
steps.description: Voice-specific call detail including all hold/mute/transfer events and DNIS routing path
Recording metadata — confirms recording was captured; links to archival policy
Actual recording objects with signed download URLs
Top-level S&TA scores including sentiment, silence, and overtalk
Topic and category classifications applied to the conversation by the S&TA engine

steps.provides: AI summary text per communication leg
steps.description: AI-generated summaries for each leg — useful for rapid content review without listening
Signed transcript download URL per communication leg (requires communicationId from seed)
Copilot/Agent Assist AI summaries — reason for contact and resolution notes
Quality evaluation scores; filter by conversationId in the query parameters
Post-interaction customer satisfaction survey results
Raw SIP signalling log — critical for trunk routing diagnosis and call failure forensics

single-conversation-investigation.joinSequence: seed → conversation-base (conversationId) → participant-wrapup (per participantId) → recording-metadata → sta-overview → evaluation (filter) → survey
voiceEngineerHighlights: sip-trace: SIP 486/503/487 responses reveal trunk capacity or routing-rule failure
call-detail: DNIS value vs. expected queue DNIS confirms correct routing table entry
recording-metadata: absent recording (empty result) indicates edge policy or consent gap
sta-overview: extreme sentiment + silence > 30% flags CX issue for QM escalation
analytics-detail: tTalkComplete=0 with tAbandon>0 confirms abandoned before agent answer

agent-investigation.description: Multi-dimensional investigation of a single agent — identity, division, skills, queue memberships, conversation history, performance aggregates, quality scores, adherence, and coaching. Used for performance reviews, PIPs, and escalation root-cause. Divisions are the cross-queue grouping unit: an agent's divisionId ties them to queues across the organisation regardless of queue configuration.

steps.provides: name, email, state, department, manager, station
steps.description: Agent identity with presence, routing status, and conversation summary
Division assignment — the primary cross-queue grouping for this agent
All routing skills assigned to the agent with proficiency levels
Queues the agent is a member of — spans all divisions
Per-channel concurrency limits — how many simultaneous chats/calls/emails this agent can handle
Presence and routing-status segments over the investigation window
Aggregated performance metrics for the window; group by userId for per-agent view

steps.provides: conversationId, queueId, wrapUpCode, tTalk, tHandle, mediaType
steps.description: All conversations the agent participated in during the window
Current presence and routing status — point-in-time only
Live active session detail — what channels this agent is handling right now
Quality evaluations received in the window; join to conversations on conversationId
Aggregate QA activity summary — faster than iterating individual evaluations for totals
WFM team membership — used to retrieve schedule and adherence data
Current schedule adherence — scheduled vs. actual state with variance
Coaching and development sessions for the agent in the window

agent-investigation.joinSequence: seed (identity) → division (id) → skills (userId) → queue-memberships (userId) → presence-activity → performance-aggregates → conversations → evaluations (agentUserId) → qa-activity-summary → wfm-management-unit → adherence
agent-investigation.divisionNote: Divisions span queues — group agents by division.id for cross-queue performance comparison. Use authorization.search.division.objects with objectType=QUEUE to enumerate all queues in a division before computing queue-scoped aggregates.

queue-investigation.description: End-to-end investigation of a queue — configuration, membership, real-time health, EWT, historical SLA, abandons, transfers, wrapup distribution, and per-agent performance within the queue. Covers both the current moment and a historical window.

steps.description: Queue configuration — routing settings, ACW timeout, and scoring method
Valid wrapup codes assigned to this queue — used to decode wrapup distribution
All queue members with live routing status and presence
Live queue counters — interacting, waiting, alerting, and on/off-queue agent counts
Real-time EWT — caller wait forecast; compare to SLA target for immediate action trigger
Live per-agent presence and routing status within this queue

steps.provides: nConnected, tHandle, tTalk, tAcw, tAnswered, nOffered
steps.description: Historical handle/talk/ACW metrics grouped by queue and media type
Service level / SLA compliance — % answered within target threshold
Abandon volume and timing — short abandons may indicate IVR issues, long abandons indicate understaffing
Transfer analysis — high blind transfers may indicate agents routing around queue rules
Wrapup code frequency — reveals call reason distribution and potential mis-classification
Full conversation list for the queue in the window — seed for per-conversation drilldown
Quality evaluations for conversations that touched this queue

queue-investigation.joinSequence: seed (queue-config) → members (queueId) → real-time-observations → estimated-wait-time → performance → sla → abandons → transfers → wrapup-distribution

division-investigation.description: Cross-queue analysis scoped to a division. Divisions are the primary organisational grouping in Genesys Cloud — agents assigned to a division serve queues in that division, even if those queues span different functional areas. Use this recipe to produce territory/region or business-unit level executive reporting.

steps.description: Division metadata — name and description
All queues assigned to this division — drives all queue-scoped aggregates below

steps.provides: userId list with name, email, state
steps.description: All agents whose primary division is this one — spans all queue memberships
Access control grants in this division — who has what permissions here

steps.provides: nConnected, tHandle, tTalk, tAcw per agent
steps.description: Performance aggregates for all agents in the division over the window

steps.provides: nConnected, tHandle, tTalk, nOffered, nError by divisionId (daily)
steps.description: Division-level conversation volume and handle-time aggregates — use as the KPI rollup

steps.provides: SLA, abandon, handle metrics per queue
steps.description: Queue-level metrics for all queues in the division — drives per-queue drilldown

steps.provides: evalCount, avgScore per agent in division
steps.description: Quality scores for all agents in the division

division-investigation.divisionNote: Agents serve queues across divisions — an agent's primary division.id does not restrict which queues they appear in. Use authorization.search.division.objects with objectType=QUEUE to enumerate the definitive queue list for a division, then use those queueIds to filter conversation and quality queries.

service-level-and-abandon-kpis.description: Weekly/monthly SLA health — service level %, abandon rate, and volume trends grouped by queue. The primary input for executive contact-centre dashboards.
service-level-and-abandon-kpis.executivePresentation: KPI cards: Service Level %, Abandon Rate %, Volume, AHT

agent-performance-scorecard.description: Per-agent handle time, volume, and quality scores across all queues in a division. Used in monthly agent scorecards, performance reviews, and PIP tracking.
agent-performance-scorecard.executivePresentation: Sortable agent scorecard table; highlight outliers on AHT and eval score

wrapup-and-transfer-analysis.description: Wrapup code distribution and transfer rates — reveals mis-classification, escalation patterns, and call routing gaps. High value for CX strategy and routing optimisation.
wrapup-and-transfer-analysis.executivePresentation: Wrapup pie chart + transfer type bar chart per queue; flag queues with blind transfer > 20%

quality-and-csat-summary.description: QM rollup — evaluation scores and CSAT/NPS completion rates per agent and queue. Feeds quality management dashboards and calibration sessions.
quality-and-csat-summary.executivePresentation: QM trend line + agent ranking table; highlight critical-item failures and low CSAT

speech-text-analytics-sentiment-trends.description: Org-wide sentiment and topic trends for voice channels. Identifies rising negativity, compliance-risk conversations, and topic spikes that require targeted coaching or process changes.
speech-text-analytics-sentiment-trends.executivePresentation: Sentiment trend chart + topic frequency heat map; flag topics with negative sentiment correlation

digital-channel-volume-and-sla.description: Omni-channel volume breakdown — compare voice, chat, email, and messaging workloads for capacity planning and SLA compliance reporting across all media types.
digital-channel-volume-and-sla.executivePresentation: Channel mix stacked bar; SLA comparison table by mediaType

wfm-adherence-and-occupancy.description: Workforce adherence and occupancy — scheduled vs. actual on-queue time. Used for WFM reports, staffing adjustment decisions, and payroll validation.
wfm-adherence-and-occupancy.executivePresentation: Adherence heat map by management unit + individual agent variance table

outbound-campaign-performance.description: Outbound dialer campaign reach rates, contact dispositions, and messaging engagement. Feeds campaign ROI reporting and list penetration tracking.
outbound-campaign-performance.executivePresentation: Campaign funnel chart + disposition breakdown pie

flow-and-ivr-performance.description: Architect IVR and bot flow execution metrics — outcome success rates, milestone completion, and self-service containment. Informs IVR design and bot deflection ROI.
flow-and-ivr-performance.executivePresentation: Containment funnel + flow outcome breakdown; compare bot vs. IVR containment rates

single-call-forensics.description: Deep-dive into one call to diagnose quality issues, misrouting, recording gaps, or unexpected disconnects. The go-to playbook for a customer escalation or complaint.
diagnosticSignals: SIP 486 Busy Here → trunk at capacity; check telephony.get.trunk.metrics.summary
SIP 503 Service Unavailable → edge or trunk provider outage
SIP 487 Request Terminated → caller hung up before answer (confirmed abandon)
SIP 4xx on INVITE with wrong DNIS → routing table misconfiguration
tTalkComplete=0 + tAbandon>0 in analytics → caller abandoned in queue before agent
recording absent (empty result from conversations.get.recordings) → edge recording policy or consent flag missing
sentimentScore < -0.5 → severe CX impact; escalate to QM for evaluation priority
ANI displayed as 'Anonymous' → CLI blocking active on inbound DID
originatingDirection=outbound on inbound DID → DNIS routing to wrong queue direction
enrichWith: users (for agent identity given userId from analytics-detail participants)

trunk-and-edge-health-check.description: SIP infrastructure health snapshot — edge appliance status, trunk utilisation, active call load, and station registration. Used for proactive monitoring and incident response.
diagnosticSignals: Edge statusCode != 'ACTIVE' → failover condition; check edge firmware and network path
Trunk inService=false → provider circuit down or PSTN gateway misconfiguration
trunk.currentCalls / trunk.maxConcurrentCalls > 0.85 → capacity saturation risk
Stations with registered=false spike → network or DNS issue affecting softphone registration
Active calls vs. trunk maxConcurrentCalls → concurrent call ceiling proximity
Edge with onlineStatus=DISCONNECTED → peer connection lost; check management network
enrichWith: alerting.get.rules (confirm alerting thresholds are configured for trunk capacity)

queue-saturation-and-staffing-analysis.description: Real-time and near-real-time analysis of queue congestion — waiting callers, on-queue agents, EWT, and historical abandon trends. Used when a queue is hot and supervisors need to act within minutes.
diagnosticSignals: oWaiting > 0 + oOnQueueUsers = 0 → no agents staffed; escalate immediately
oOffQueueUsers high relative to oOnQueueUsers → agents logged in but not ready (presence audit needed)
EWT > SLA target → real-time staffing intervention required
nAbandoned spike in last hour → callers abandoning; correlate with EWT to confirm cause
oInteracting / oOnQueueUsers > 0.9 → agents fully occupied; queue will build
presence = 'On Queue' but routingStatus = 'NOT_RESPONDING' → ghost agents; check station registration
enrichWith: users (to resolve userId to name for supervisor view)
workforce.get.management.unit.adherence (to see if off-queue agents are on a scheduled break)

flow-and-ivr-diagnostics.description: Architect IVR and bot flow execution health — identifying flows with high failure rates, stuck executions, or unexpected exit paths. Used for post-deployment validation and incident root-cause on self-service outages.
diagnosticSignals: nFlowOutcomeFailed spike → IVR routing error, missing menu option, or backend API timeout
nFlowMilestone count lower than baseline → callers abandoning mid-flow (check hold music / wait prompts)
oFlow high with zero queue activity → calls stuck in flow loop or dead-end branch
Flow outcome = 'Default Exit' > 5% → unmatched input causing fallthrough to default branch
Bot flow nFlowOutcomeFailed / nFlow > 0.10 → NLU intent model degradation; retest utterances
nFlow drop without corresponding queue drop → calls not reaching the flow (DID routing issue)
enrichWith: analytics.query.conversation.details.today (to correlate flow failures to specific conversations)

recording-compliance-audit.description: Verify recording coverage across a queue or agent — confirm recordings are being captured per policy. Used for compliance audits and archival verification.
diagnosticSignals: conversationId in details but absent from recordings → recording policy not triggering; check queue recording rules
recording.fileState = 'ERROR' → edge storage issue or retention policy conflict
recording.mediaType != conversation.mediaType → wrong recording profile applied
archiveDate in past with no archiveDestination → archival export job misconfigured
recording.duration << conversation.tTalk → recording stopped early (agent disconnection or hold gap)
enrichWith: audit-logs (filter by service=Recording to see policy change events)
alerting.get.rules (verify a recording-gap alert rule is configured)
